title: Workbench feature surface â€” capability Ă— tool matrix for Web Docs
summary: The cross-tabbed feature surface (capability Ă— tool) over the workbench/docs-tool landscape from #624 â€” stage 2 of the Web Docs feature-pipeline epic #623, raw material the derivation decision #626 classifies. Registry: src/_data/workbenchFeatures.json (20 features in 7 groups: render, authoring, controls, testing, theming, discovery, distribution). Each feature records which landscape tools ship it, ubiquity (table-stakesâ†’niche), a one-line 'what a WE-native version would need', and the load-bearing weStatus column that pre-classifies it for #626: reuse-existing / extend-existing / new-surface / out-of-scope. The decisive finding: roughly two thirds of the surface already maps to standards WE has. REUSE-EXISTING â€” viewportâ†’breakpoint intent, theming + token-tablesâ†’webtheme(+data-table), a11y panelâ†’webcompliance, navigation/IAâ†’nav-list/tree-select/breadcrumb, i18nâ†’webintl, analyticsâ†’webanalytics, access-control/meteredâ†’access-control+web-identity (this is what powers #398 open-core tiering), extensibilityâ†’webplugs/webregistries. EXTEND-EXISTING â€” args/controlsâ†’Technical Configurator fed by CEM, stories/states + interaction-testsâ†’webcases overlap, long-form docsâ†’webediting authoring, doc versioningâ†’upgrader/webadapters. GENUINELY NEW surface (the small core #626 must actually design) â€” the component-isolation renderer, CEM-driven autodocs (the #624 emit prior; api-viewer is the zero-build proof), full-text search index, source/code view. OUT-OF-SCOPE for the standard â€” visual regression (a CI/hosted service â†’ #398 hosted tier, Chromatic-paid vs Lost-Pixel-OSS) and the Figma designâ†”code bridge (design-tool integration; its fully-hosted form, Backlight, already failed). Net: #626 is mostly a dedupe-and-compose exercise, not a greenfield mint. Full grounding in reports/2026-06-14-component-workbench-landscape.md (landscape) + this matrix.
